skill.name: Pexo AI Video Agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5-120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music to produce short-form videos.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found 534 pattern matches across shell command execution, network calls, filesystem access, and env variable usage. All patterns are FALSE POSITIVES in context - they are legitimate operations for a shell-script-based video agent that wraps the Pexo API. The scripts execute curl commands to Pexo's official API endpoints, read a designated config file at ~/.pexo/config, and access required environment variables (PEXO_API_KEY, PEXO_BASE_URL). The associated heuristic detection of code-execution-plus-credential-access is expected behavior for an API-wrapping tool. The skill uses a mask_secret function to safely display API keys in diagnostic output. Risk level set to MEDIUM because the combination of external commands, network access, filesystem reads, and credential access creates a legitimate risk profile that users should understand, even though no malicious intent or data exfiltration patterns were detected.

medium_findings.title: External command execution in shell scripts
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Shell command substitution patterns ($(...) and backtick) detected in .sh scripts and markdown docs. These are expected in executable shell scripts that form the skill's core functionality. Commands are curl (to Pexo API), jq (JSON parsing), and standard file utilities. No unvalidated user input is injected into command strings, so no command injection risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All shell command executions are within executable scripts using standard patterns. Command arguments are hardcoded or derived from controlled API responses, not from untrusted user input.

medium_findings.title: Hidden file in home directory
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: References to ~/.pexo/config and similar dot-files are the designated configuration mechanism documented in the skill's requirements section. Reading a config file from the home directory is standard and expected behavior for CLI tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The config file path is a documented requirement of the skill. The pattern ~/.pexo/config is the explicit configuration mechanism, not an attempt to access arbitrary hidden files.

medium_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm references
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: References to 'sk-' prefix in API key documentation and usage examples are standard API key format descriptions, not implementations of weak cryptographic algorithms. The scanner pattern-matched the string 'sk-' as a cryptographic reference.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Only documentation and API key format validation are present. No actual cryptographic operations are performed in this codebase.

medium_findings.title: Environmental variable access for API keys
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL are required configuration variables explicitly documented in the skill manifest. The skill needs these environment variables to authenticate with the Pexo video creation API. The diagnostic script masks the key value in output using mask_secret().
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Env access is explicitly declared in the skill's requires block and is necessary for the skill to function. The diagnostic script takes care to mask the secret value in user-facing output.

medium_findings.title: Dangerous combination pattern: code execution, network, credential access
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE - Heuristic: The combination of external commands + network access + credential access is expected for an API-wrapping CLI tool. This skill runs shell scripts that use curl to make authenticated requests to Pexo's official API (pexo.ai). This is a legitimate architecture pattern for API-integrated tools, not malicious behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network requests target pexo.ai domains. Credentials are used only for Bearer auth headers. No external or unknown destinations are contacted. Pattern is standard for API-wrapping tools.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: URLs hardcoded in SKILL.md and reference docs point to pexo.ai, the legitimate service domain. These are documentation links for user guidance (setup guide, project page, help center).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to pexo.ai domains and are used as documentation links or API base URLs. No suspicious or unknown destinations detected.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance commands
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Commands like uname, which, and file --version are used in the pexo-doctor.sh diagnostic script to verify system requirements. These are legitimate environment checks, not reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: System information commands are used exclusively in a diagnostic tool that checks prerequisites. No exfiltration of system information occurs.

low_findings.title: Windows SAM database reference
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static scanner misidentified content at SKILL.md:43 as a Windows SAM database reference. The actual content at that location is the language rule instruction: 'You MUST reply to the user in the SAME language they use.'
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 43 contains a language instruction, not a reference to any system security database. Clear scanner false positive.

low_findings.title: Temp file creation in shell scripts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: mktemp is used in shell scripts to create temporary files for storing API response bodies and HTTP headers. Temp files are cleaned up with rm -f after processing. This is a standard pattern in shell scripting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All temp files are created with mktemp and cleaned up in the same function. No sensitive data is written to temp files beyond API response payloads.

low_findings.title: Temp directory access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The pexo_tmp_dir function uses ~/.pexo/tmp as a configurable temp directory. This is documented behavior within the skill's own config directory. No access to system /tmp or other temp locations occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The temp directory path is configurable via PEXO_TMP_DIR and defaults to a subdirectory under the skill's own config directory. This is a standard sandboxing pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command execution via curl with API authentication
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill's shell scripts use curl to make authenticated HTTP requests to the Pexo API. This is the intended operational pattern - the skill wraps the Pexo video creation API. All requests use Bearer token authentication via PEXO_API_KEY.

dangerous_patterns.title: Config file parsing from home directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads configuration from ~/.pexo/config which contains API credentials. This is the designated and documented configuration mechanism. The file is sourced (not executed as a script) and only expected variables are accessed.

dangerous_patterns.title: File upload to external service
dangerous_patterns.description: The pexo-upload.sh script uploads user-provided media files to Pexo's cloud storage via an upload credential workflow. Files are uploaded directly to Pexo's infrastructure (pexo.ai / Alibaba Cloud OSS) for video processing.

content.user_title: Create AI videos using the Pexo video agent
content.value_statement: Making short-form videos for social media, products, or brand content usually requires complex editing tools and creative skills. This skill connects you to the Pexo AI video agent so you can describe your video idea and get a finished video in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Create short videos (5-120 seconds) from a text description
Upload and use reference images, videos, and audio in video projects
Make revisions to existing videos by sending new instructions
Poll for video production status and deliver the final result
Support aspect ratios 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 for different platforms
Handle credit checks and provide top-up instructions when needed
limitations: Cannot create videos longer than 120 seconds
Requires an active Pexo account with API key and sufficient credits
Video production takes 15-20 minutes (cannot be delivered instantly)
Cannot rewrite video scripts or add creative details beyond what the user says

use_cases.title: Product promotional video
use_cases.description: A marketing professional describes their product and uploads photos. The skill creates a short promotional video with script, shots, and music.

use_cases.title: Social media content
use_cases.description: A content creator asks for a TikTok or Instagram Reel about a topic. The skill produces a platform-ready short video.

use_cases.title: Explainer video
use_cases.description: A teacher or trainer describes a concept they want to explain visually. The skill creates a short educational video.

prompt_templates.title: Simple video creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 15-second promotional video about my new coffee shop.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use for a quick video

prompt_templates.title: Video with reference files
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product launch video for my running shoes. I have uploaded three photos and one video clip to use as reference.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a video with user-provided media assets

prompt_templates.title: Video revision
prompt_templates.prompt: I have project proj_abc123. Change the ending to show the logo for 3 more seconds and use a softer background music.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting changes to an existing video project

prompt_templates.title: Multi-platform video creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a vertical 9:16 video about my new app launch. Duration 20 seconds. Show the main features in the first 10 seconds and the download button at the end.
prompt_templates.scenario: Specifying aspect ratio and timing for a specific social platform

output_examples.input: Make a 10-second ad for my handmade soap business. I am uploading three product photos.
output: Message sent to Pexo: 'Make a 10-second ad for my handmade soap business'
After 15 minutes: Video delivered with download link and preview

output_examples.input: I want to change the music in my existing video project proj_xyz789.
output: After production: Updated video delivered with the new music track

best_practices: Describe the video goal clearly but keep the message short. Pexo handles the creative details.
Upload reference images before sending the video description so the agent can include them.
Wait at least 60 seconds between status checks to avoid duplicate video production.
anti_patterns: Do not add video duration, style, or camera directions that the user did not ask for. This overrides Pexo's professional judgment.
Do not send multiple separate messages to Pexo about the same video. Consolidate all information into one message.
Do not send bare asset IDs without XML tags. They must be wrapped in <original-image>, <original-video>, or <original-audio> tags.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a Pexo account with an API key. Set up the config file at ~/.pexo/config with PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL.

faq.question: How long does it take to make a video?
faq.answer: A typical 15-second video takes about 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos take more time.

faq.question: What video formats and sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Output videos are 5-120 seconds long. Supported aspect ratios are 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait), and 1:1 (square).

faq.question: Can I use my own images and videos?
faq.answer: Yes. Upload images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), videos (mp4, mov, avi), or audio (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac).

faq.question: Can I make changes to a video after it is created?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project ID and send your revision request. Pexo will process your changes.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits?
faq.answer: The skill will detect insufficient credits and guide you to the Pexo website where you can purchase more credits.
